report_name: New 'Bad Epoll' Linux Kernel Zero-Day (CVE-2026-46242) Grants Full Root Access

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: A local unprivileged user can exploit the 'Bad Epoll' use-after-free vulnerability (CVE-2026-46242) to execute arbitrary code with kernel privileges, gaining full root access. The exploit can be chained with other vulnerabilities, such as a browser RCE, for a full system compromise from a sandboxed environment.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-46242
vulnerabilities.description: A high-severity use-after-free (UAF) vulnerability in the epoll event notification subsystem of the Linux kernel, specifically in the ep_remove() function. It allows a local unprivileged attacker to trigger a race condition to achieve arbitrary code execution with kernel privileges, leading to full root access.

indicators.name: Unexpected Root Process Spawned by Unprivileged User
indicators.pattern:
title: Unexpected Root Process Spawned by Unprivileged User
id: 2b9f3c1a-8d7e-4f6a-9b1c-5d3e2f1a0b9c
status: experimental
description: Detects a process running as root that was spawned by an unprivileged user or service account, which is a strong indicator of a successful local privilege escalation exploit like CVE-2026-46242 (Bad Epoll).
references:
  - https://cybersecuritynews.com/bad-epoll-0-day-vulnerability/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-05
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    User: 'root'
  filter_legit_parent:
    ParentUser:
      - 'root'
      - 'system'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_parent
indicators.description: Detects a process running as root that was spawned by an unprivileged user or service account. This is a strong indicator of a successful local privilege escalation exploit like the 'Bad Epoll' vulnerability.

indicators.name: Linux Kernel Use-After-Free Log Message
indicators.pattern:
title: Linux Kernel Use-After-Free Log Message
id: 8e4d2c1b-9a0f-4e8d-a1b3-c2d1e0f9a8b7
status: experimental
description: Detects kernel log messages indicating a potential use-after-free condition, which could be a sign of an attempted or successful exploitation of a kernel vulnerability like 'Bad Epoll' (CVE-2026-46242).
references:
  - https://cybersecuritynews.com/bad-epoll-0-day-vulnerability/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-05
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: medium
logsource:
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    keywords:
      - 'use-after-free'
      - 'epoll'
  condition: all of selection
indicators.description: Detects kernel log messages for 'use-after-free' and 'epoll' which may indicate an attempt to exploit CVE-2026-46242.

relationships.source_name: Unexpected Root Process Spawned by Unprivileged User
